element_introduction_order.element: Grab mechanics (body slam, pile driver, atomic back drop)
Monster roster (Geon, Rocky, Beetle Mania, Astro Guy)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players encounter same monsters twice in different cities

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental destruction (kicking over skyscrapers and stadiums)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Visual feedback system integrated into combat
Culmination of mastery over all mechanics before second loop

element_introduction_order.element: City progression (Tokyo, Kyoto, Okayama, Osaka, Kobe, Hiroshima)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cosmetic variation; same mechanics applied in different environments

typical_relief_triggers: Successful pin for three count victory
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Repetitive tension structure: 12 total rounds (8 in ports) featuring same 6 monsters fought twice in succession, creating escalating familiarity rather than novelty-driven intensity

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory encounters with basic monster roster establish punch/kick/grab mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Same monsters encountered again with increased familiarity but in different city settings
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mirror match against player character (palette-swapped variant) serves as gauntlet requiring complete mastery of all combat mechanics
notable_spikes: Final round mirror match represents difficulty spike requiring demonstration of full mechanical mastery

notable_level_patterns.description: 12 rounds total (8 in 16-bit ports): first loop defeats all 6 monsters sequentially, second loop repeats the same 6 monsters in same order but different cities. Creates familiarity-based progression rather than escalating enemy complexity.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tokyo Round 1 (first encounter with Geon) vs Tokyo Round 7 (second encounter with Geon)

notable_level_patterns.description: Battles take place in 6 Japanese cities with game beginning and ending in Tokyo. Genesis port modifies this to feature Tokyo repeatedly with thematic variants (Mega Port, Dragon City, Castle City). Cosmetic variety masks mechanically identical encounters.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Kyoto vs Okayama vs Osaka vs Kobe vs Hiroshima (all feature same monster matchups with different backdrops)

notable_level_patterns.description: Last monster of first loop is the player's own character in different palette, serving as mirror match. Tests complete mastery of mechanics before entering second loop.
